Open - Load a Cubemap, Panorama, or 6 images to stitch Layout - Switch the current projection type (Auto Detect, Horizontal, Vertical) Output - Displays the output folder where files will be saved. '...' - Select an output folder. Export - The two export buttons in the upper right will change depending on the current loaded Cubemap or Panorama image. The dark vertical bar between the two preview areas can be dragged and resized. Splitting a Cubemap / Panorama ------------------------------ Press the 'Open' button at the top right of the program. The Cubemap Splitter can load *.jpg, *.jpeg, *.bmp, *.png, or *.hdr Horizontal Cubemaps, Vertical Cubemaps, or Panoramas. When loading an .hdr image, normalizing and tone mapping will be applied to compress the high dynamic range of values to better display them in the program. license_snippets: Readme.txt: Isometric Stone Soup by Screaming Brain Studios (CC0) This pack has been converted from the Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup (CC0) tileset collection. Original Source: https://opengameart.org/content/dungeon-crawl-32x32-tiles-supplemental Wall Tiles ========== There are *.tsx tile-sheets for Tiled Map Editor for every tileset, which lets you mix and match tiles as needed. All Block tile-sheets also contain flat "Top Face" tiles which can be used as overlays on top of walls. These are found right beneath each of the Full and Half block tiles in every tile-sheet. This allows for hiding the top of certain blocks that may have decorations or features you only wish to be displayed on the sides of the block.
